title: adopt .pddignore + lowercase at_todo: scan coverage as editable config
notes: Infra/DEV. Overran C (25m vs 12m, 2.08x) — building was quick but the AT_TODO->at_todo discovery forced a redesign mid-task: pdd matches the uppercase keyword as a SUBSTRING (AT_TODO aborts the scan), so the placeholder had to drop to lowercase, verified empirically in a temp dir. NOTE: actual_min is SUMMED hands-on time; wall-clock (started->finished) spans a multi-hour compaction/idle gap and is NOT the work duration. Behavior-preserving vs old hardcoded excludes (same 1-puzzle set). Worktree.

title: formalize 12 informal demo TODOs as @todo #143/#144; un-ignore demos/
notes: Big underrun (4.65m vs C 15m, 0.31x). Mechanical formalization: created 2 grouping trackers (#143 gameSnake features, #144 research Qs), converted 12 TODO:->@todo via line-addressed sed (.a/.ap can't use Edit tool — treated as binary), dropped demos/**+plusdemos/** from .pddignore. C was padded for risk that pdd wouldn't scan .a/.ap or the format would fight the parser; verified up front in a temp dir that it does, then conversion was trivial. Diff proven comment-only (assembly unchanged). Scan 1->13 puzzles, exit 0. Closes the #141 follow-up. Worktree.

title: Spike: debugger report-artifact parity vs oracle (lst/bst × .a/.e)
notes: Spike under #13. Built a reusable 2x2 probe (experiments/debugger-report-parity-probe.sh) running oracle×lccjs over run-on-.a/run-on-.e, snapshotting all 8 artifacts + diffing. Finding: NO missing artifact (the remembered symptom did not reproduce, incl. under -d — both write .lst+.bst everywhere; corrected a misread that -d suppresses stats: line364 generateStats=false is under -nostats, not -d). Real diffs are 6 formatting deltas (header A/S padding, stats alignment, src/code column geometry, .a source-line truncation [only content-affecting], BST trailing space, banner date fmt). Reframes #13 to report-format parity; decomposition posted to #13. Ratio 0.38x (5.77m vs C15m) — fast: oracle wired + warm context. In-repo close.

title: validate lccjs-assembly skill — first-pass via 6 fresh subagents
notes: first TEST-role puzzle. Fan-out 6 parallel subagents (3 positive + 3 negative); I assembled + ran each, scored against design §6. 5/6 assembled and ran; all 4 pitfall classes steered correctly. Single failure (N3) on a different axis (invented `puts r0`) — filed as follow-up #148. Writeup: docs/lccjs-assembly-skill-validation.md. Closes the #115->#116->#117 skill arc. Ratio 3.31x C over-pad — TEST flavor with parallel subagents was faster than predicted; the orchestration overhead I budgeted didn't manifest because subagents ran truly in parallel and writeup leaned on direct observation. Closes #117.
